annotations.label: Analog computer

anchor.snippet: An analog computer or analogue computer is a type of computation machine

annotations.note: Analog computers are physical computing devices, not a mathematical concept formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Digital computer (contrast)

anchor.snippet: digital computers represent varying quantities symbolically and by discrete values of both time and amplitude

annotations.note: Digital computer as a hardware concept is not a Mathlib subject.

annotations.label: Antikythera mechanism

anchor.snippet: The Antikythera mechanism , a type of device used to determine the positions of heavenly bodies known as an orrery

annotations.note: Historical mechanical device, not a Mathlib formalization target.

anchor.snippet: the astrolabe was effectively an analog computer capable of working out several different kinds of problems in spherical astronomy

annotations.label: Planimeter

anchor.snippet: The planimeter was a manual instrument to calculate the area of a closed figure by tracing over it with a mechanical linkage.

annotations.note: The planimeter as device is not formalized; its underlying Green's theorem is in Mathlib but not referenced here.

annotations.label: Slide rule

anchor.snippet: It is a hand-operated analog computer for doing multiplication and division.

annotations.note: Physical computing instrument, not a Mathlib concept.

annotations.label: Differential analyser

anchor.snippet: The differential analyser , a mechanical analog computer designed to solve differential equations by integration

annotations.note: Historical mechanical ODE solver; not formalized.

annotations.label: Spring-mass system on analog computer

anchor.snippet: the dynamics of a spring-mass system can be described by the equation

annotations.note: The specific spring-mass ODE m x'' + b x' + k x = 0 is not stated as a named result in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiplication via similar right triangles

anchor.snippet: Multiplication was done by mechanisms based on the geometry of similar right triangles.

annotations.note: Mechanical multiplication technique; not a Mathlib theorem.

annotations.label: Polar-to-rectangular mechanical resolver

anchor.snippet: Coordinate conversion from polar to rectangular was done by a mechanical resolver

annotations.note: Refers to a mechanical device; the mathematical polar-to-rectangular map exists in Mathlib (Complex.polarCoord) but the article statement is about the device.

annotations.label: Mechanical integrator (disc and wheel)

anchor.snippet: Integration with respect to another variable was done by a rotating disc driven by one variable.

annotations.note: Mechanical integrator hardware, not a Mathlib subject.

anchor.snippet: Op amps are a particular type of feedback amplifier with very high gain and stable input
The majority of op amps in a representative setup are summing amplifiers, which add and subtract analog voltages

annotations.label: Op-amp integrator with respect to time

anchor.snippet: op amps with capacitor feedback are usually included in a setup; they integrate the sum of their inputs with respect to time

annotations.note: The circuit is not formalized; integration with respect to a variable exists in Mathlib generally but not as a model of this device.

annotations.label: Transistor junction as log/exp function

anchor.snippet: the forward voltage drop of a transistor's base-emitter junction can provide a usably accurate logarithmic or exponential function

annotations.note: Semiconductor physics fact; not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spaghetti sort and other physical models

anchor.snippet: using a bundle of spaghetti as a model of sorting numbers

annotations.note: Pedagogical physical metaphor; not a Mathlib concept.

annotations.label: Division via multiplier in feedback path

anchor.snippet: Division is carried out with a multiplier in the feedback path of an Operational Amplifier.

annotations.note: Circuit-engineering claim; not a Mathlib theorem.

annotations.label: Differentiation avoided due to high-frequency noise

anchor.snippet: It corresponds in the frequency domain to a high-pass filter, which means that high-frequency noise is amplified
